scenario: Pinnacle Bank schedules 9000 loans per month in Camunda 8.8. A FEEL expression on a process timer must compute the renewal due date as the last day of the month of each contract, where contract.startedAt is supplied as a date and time value such as 2022-10-16T12:00:00, drawing on the FEEL temporal built-ins.
question: What does last day of month(date and time("2022-10-16T12:00:00")) return when used to compute the renewal due date in this FEEL expression?

options.text: It returns the date value 2022-10-31, representing the final calendar day of the month that contains the supplied input value
It returns the date-time value 2022-10-31T12:00:00, preserving the original time component on the last day of the input month
It returns the number 31, representing how many days are contained in the calendar month of the supplied input date value
It returns the date value 2022-11-01, representing the first day after the last day of the month containing the input value

a.text: Correct. The function takes the month of the supplied date or date and time value and returns the last day of that month as a date, so the documented date and time 2022-10-16T12:00:00 yields the date value 2022-10-31 exactly as shown in the FEEL temporal built-ins example.

b.text: Incorrect. The documented signature returns a date and not a date and time value, so the original time component is dropped and the result for the October input is the date 2022-10-31 rather than 2022-10-31T12:00:00.

c.text: Incorrect. The function returns a date value rather than a numeric day count, so although October contains 31 days, the documented result is the date value 2022-10-31 and not the standalone number 31 by itself.

d.text: Incorrect. The function returns the last day of the current month and not the first day of the following month, so the documented result for an October input is 2022-10-31 and not the date 2022-11-01.

explanation: last day of month takes the month of the supplied date or date and time value and returns the last day of that month as a date, so an October input such as 2022-10-16T12:00:00 evaluates to the date value 2022-10-31 regardless of the original time component.
